special_mechanics.name: Long Jump
special_mechanics.description: Running by alternating button presses with correct timing for jump. Hold jump button to set angle. 42 degrees documented as optimal angle.

special_mechanics.name: Javelin Throw
special_mechanics.description: Running by alternating button presses then using action button for correct timing and angle selection. 43 degrees documented as optimal.

special_mechanics.name: Hammer Throw
special_mechanics.description: Spinning initiated by pressing run button once, then correctly timed action button press to choose release angle. 45 degrees documented as optimal.

special_mechanics.name: High Jump
special_mechanics.description: Running at computer-set speed, then action button held down to determine jump angle. Once airborne, run button can be rapidly pressed for additional height.

special_mechanics.name: 110 Meter Hurdles
special_mechanics.description: Running by alternating button presses with action button timing jumps over hurdles.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. No numeric physics parameters documented in sources. Game design focuses on button input mechanics rather than detailed physics simulation. Movement is controlled entirely through button-press intensity and timing for different athletic events.
